req.title: Upload onboarding document — variant 5
req.statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three key axes: (1) file size limit of 10 MB, (2) MIME type allow-list (PDF, PNG, JPG), and (3) 2FA confirmation gate. EP partitions valid/invalid MIME types and valid/invalid sizes. BVA targets the exact 10 MB boundary (10485760 bytes). NEG covers oversized and wrong-type files. STC covers the 2FA state transition (unauthenticated-2FA → authenticated-2FA → upload permitted). DT captures the combined size×type decision space. Minimum suite: one positive (valid PDF ≤10 MB + 2FA), one negative (oversized file → 413, wrong MIME → 415), one boundary (exactly 10 MB PDF). Additional cases cover 2FA state and MIME rejection to achieve adequate coverage.

tests.name: test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_with_2fa
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid session token
2FA is enabled and TOTP secret is available
A valid PDF file of 1 MB exists on the test runner
actions: Generate a 1 MB in-memory PDF payload
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with the PDF and 2FA token
expected: Response body indicates virus_scan status is queued
trace: R-WEB-081: valid file type PDF accepted
R-WEB-081: file size within 10 MB limit accepted
R-WEB-081: document stored and virus scan queued
tests.executable:
import pytest
import pyotp

def test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_with_2fa(auth_session, totp_secret, make_file):
    file_bytes = make_file(size_bytes=1 * 1024 * 1024, mime="application/pdf", ext="pdf")
    totp_token = pyotp.TOTP(totp_secret).now()
    response = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("onboarding.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")},
        data={"totp_token": totp_token},
    )
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "document_id missing from response"
    assert body.get("virus_scan") == "queued", "Virus scan not queued"

tests.name: test_upload_file_exceeding_10mb_returns_413
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid session token
An oversized PDF file of 11 MB is available
actions: Generate an 11 MB in-memory PDF payload (10485761+ bytes)
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with the oversized PDF
expected: Response body contains error code indicating payload too large
trace: R-WEB-081: file size > 10 MB must be rejected with 413
tests.executable:
import pytest
import pyotp

def test_upload_file_exceeding_10mb_returns_413(auth_session, totp_secret, make_file):
    oversized_bytes = make_file(size_bytes=11 * 1024 * 1024, mime="application/pdf", ext="pdf")
    totp_token = pyotp.TOTP(totp_secret).now()
    response = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("big.pdf", oversized_bytes, "application/pdf")},
        data={"totp_token": totp_token},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 413, f"Expected 413, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, "No error detail in 413 response"

tests.name: test_upload_exactly_10mb_pdf_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid session token
A PDF file of exactly 10485760 bytes (10 MiB) can be generated
actions: Generate an in-memory PDF payload of exactly 10485760 bytes
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with the exact-boundary PDF
expected: Response body indicates virus_scan is queued
trace: R-WEB-081: file at exactly 10 MB boundary must be accepted
R-WEB-081: BVA upper boundary — 10485760 bytes is the max allowed size
tests.executable:
import pytest
import pyotp

MAX_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024  # 10485760

def test_upload_exactly_10mb_pdf_accepted(auth_session, totp_secret, make_file):
    boundary_bytes = make_file(size_bytes=MAX_BYTES, mime="application/pdf", ext="pdf")
    assert len(boundary_bytes) == MAX_BYTES, "Fixture must produce exact size"
    totp_token = pyotp.TOTP(totp_secret).now()
    response = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("boundary.pdf", boundary_bytes, "application/pdf")},
        data={"totp_token": totp_token},
    )
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), f"Boundary file rejected: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "document_id" in body
    assert body.get("virus_scan") == "queued"

tests.name: test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid session token
A valid EXE file under 10 MB is available for upload
actions: Generate a 512 KB in-memory payload declared as application/octet-stream with .exe extension
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with the disallowed MIME type
expected: Response body references unsupported media type or invalid format
trace: R-WEB-081: MIME type not in allow-list must be rejected with 415
R-WEB-081: error pathway — mime not in allow-list → 415
R-WEB-081: DT row — size OK, mime INVALID → reject
tests.executable:
import pytest
import pyotp

def test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415(auth_session, totp_secret, make_file):
    bad_mime_bytes = make_file(size_bytes=512 * 1024, mime="application/octet-stream", ext="exe")
    totp_token = pyotp.TOTP(totp_secret).now()
    response = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("malware.exe", bad_mime_bytes, "application/octet-stream")},
        data={"totp_token": totp_token},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 415, f"Expected 415, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, "No error detail in 415 response"

tests.name: test_upload_without_2fa_token_is_rejected
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid session token
2FA challenge has NOT been completed (no TOTP token supplied)
actions: Generate a valid 1 MB PDF payload
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload WITHOUT a totp_token field
expected: Response body indicates 2FA confirmation is required
trace: R-WEB-081: 2FA confirmation is mandatory for upload
R-WEB-081: STC — state unauthenticated-2FA cannot transition to upload-complete
R-WEB-081: missing 2FA token must block the action
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_without_2fa_token_is_rejected(auth_session, make_file):
    file_bytes = make_file(size_bytes=1 * 1024 * 1024, mime="application/pdf", ext="pdf")
    response = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("onboarding.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")},
        # Intentionally omitting totp_token
    )
    assert response.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"Expected 401 or 403 without 2FA, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "2fa" in error_text or "two-factor" in error_text or "otp" in error_text or "authentication" in error_text

tests.name: test_upload_valid_png_and_jpg_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid session token
Valid PNG and JPG files under 10 MB are available
actions: Generate a 500 KB PNG payload and POST to /documents/upload with valid TOTP
Generate a 500 KB JPG payload and POST to /documents/upload with valid TOTP
expected: Both responses contain document_id and virus_scan queued
trace: R-WEB-081: PNG is in the allowed MIME type list
R-WEB-081: JPG/JPEG is in the allowed MIME type list
R-WEB-081: EP valid partition — allowed MIME types all accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest
import pyotp

@pytest.mark.parametrize("mime,ext,label", [
    ("image/png", "png", "PNG"),
    ("image/jpeg", "jpg", "JPG"),
])
def test_upload_valid_png_and_jpg_accepted(auth_session, totp_secret, make_file, mime, ext, label):
    file_bytes = make_file(size_bytes=500 * 1024, mime=mime, ext=ext)
    totp_token = pyotp.TOTP(totp_secret).now()
    response = auth_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": (f"doc.{ext}", file_bytes, mime)},
        data={"totp_token": totp_token},
    )
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), f"{label} upload failed: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, f"{label}: document_id missing"
    assert body.get("virus_scan") == "queued", f"{label}: virus scan not queued"
